etymology.description: In this word, 串(串) (chuàn, "skewer") is the main concept of skewering food; repeated to 串串(串串) (chuànchuàn, "a series of skewers"), and 香(香) (xiāng, "fragrant") refers to the aroma from cooking or seasoning, describing the aromatic quality of the dish.
etymology.interpretation: 串串(串串) (chuànchuàn, "a series of skewers") signifies multiple skewered items, a defining characteristic of this dining style where small portions are cooked on skewers. 香(香) (xiāng, "fragrant") emphasizes the appealing scent released as the ingredients cook in the hot pot broth, drawing people in with its aroma. This describes a type of hot pot where individual skewers of ingredients are cooked in a communal pot, appealing both visually and aromatically.

examples.chinese: 我们昨天晚上去吃了串串香(我們昨天晚上去吃了串串香)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zuótiān wǎnshàng qù chī le chuàn chuàn xiāng.
examples.english: We went to eat skewered hot pot last night.

examples.chinese: 小李特别喜欢成都的串串香(小李特別喜歡成都的串串香)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo Lǐ tèbié xǐhuān Chéngdū de chuàn chuàn xiāng.
examples.english: Xiao Li especially likes Chengdu's skewered hot pot.

examples.chinese: 串串香的汤底有很多种口味(串串香的湯底有很多種口味)，可以根据自己的喜好选择(可以根據自己的喜好選擇)。
examples.pinyin: Chuàn chuàn xiāng de tāngdǐ yǒu hěn duō zhǒng kǒuwèi, kěyǐ gēnjù zìjǐ de xǐhào xuǎnzé.
examples.english: The skewered hot pot has many flavors of broth to choose from according to your preference.
